support.title: Support
support.description: Find answers to common questions or get help from our community.

whatIsPrompt.question: What is an MCP resource?
whatIsPrompt.answer: An MCP resource is a service, tool, or capability registered in the MCP (Model Context Protocol) ecosystem. Resources can be discovered and connected to AI agents to extend their abilities with external tools and data sources.

whyPromptsMatter.question: Why do MCP resources matter?
whyPromptsMatter.answer:
MCP resources enable AI systems to access external tools, data sources, and services through a standardized protocol. They become critical when:

• Building AI applications that need to interact with external systems
• Working with specialized tools that extend AI capabilities
• Creating production systems where reliability and interoperability matter

Well-configured MCP resources help you build more powerful, reliable AI-powered applications.

whatIsWebmcpland.question: What is webmcp.land?
whatIsWebmcpland.answer: webmcp.land is a community-driven registry where people share, discover, and connect MCP resources. Users can register their MCP services and tools, making them discoverable for the entire community. Free and open source.

howToUse.question: How do I use resources from this registry?
howToUse.answer: Simply browse the registry, find a resource you need, and copy its endpoint URL or configuration. You can then use it in your AI applications, Claude Desktop, VS Code, or any MCP-compatible client.

license.question: Can I use these resources commercially?
license.answer: Yes! All resources on webmcp.land are released under the Apache-2.0 (Apache License 2.0) license, which means they are open source. You can use, modify, and distribute them freely for any purpose, including commercial use, without attribution.

selfHost.question: Can I self-host this platform?
selfHost.answer: Absolutely! webmcp.land is fully open source. You can deploy your own private instance for your team or organization. Check out our self-hosting documentation for setup instructions.

verification.question: How can I become a verified user?
verification.answer: Verification is granted by administrators to users who consistently share quality resources. There are no strict rules — if you contribute valuable resources to the community, an admin may select you as verified. Focus on creating helpful, well-crafted resources and the recognition will follow.

aiCredits.question: How do AI preview generation credits work?
aiCredits.answer: Each user receives 3 AI generation credits per day by default. These credits allow you to generate preview images, videos, or audio for your resources using AI. Administrators can adjust the daily credit limit for individual users if needed.

attribution.question: What if the attribution on a resource is wrong?
attribution.answer: If you notice incorrect attribution on a resource (for example, if you're the original author but not credited), please open an issue on our GitHub repository. You can use the form below to submit your issue, and we'll review and correct it as soon as possible.

resourceWritingGuide.title: How to Create Great Resources
resourceWritingGuide.subtitle: Tips, examples, and best practices for creating effective MCP resources

interactiveBanner.title: Want a More Detailed & Interactive Experience?
interactiveBanner.description: Dive deeper with our comprehensive interactive guide featuring 25 chapters, hands-on exercises, and real-world examples to master AI prompting.

beSpecific.title: Be Specific and Clear
beSpecific.description: Vague resources lead to vague responses. Specify exactly what you want, including format, length, tone, and any constraints.

provideContext.title: Provide Context
provideContext.description: Give background information that helps the AI understand your needs. Include who, what, why, and for whom.

defineFormat.title: Define the Output Format
defineFormat.description: Specify how you want the response structured: bullet points, paragraphs, code blocks, tables, etc.

setConstraints.title: Set Constraints
setConstraints.description: Include limitations like word count, reading level, things to avoid, or specific requirements to follow.

includeExamples.title: Include Examples
includeExamples.description: Show the AI what good output looks like. Examples help calibrate the response style and quality.

rolePlaying.title: Role-Playing: "Act As" Patterns
rolePlaying.description: One of the most powerful prompting techniques is assigning the AI a specific role or persona. This helps establish expertise, tone, and perspective.

variables.title: Using Variables for Dynamic Resources
variables.description: Variables make your resources reusable and customizable. Users can fill in different values each time they use your resource.
variables.tip1: Use descriptive variable names: '$'{topic} is better than '$'{x}
variables.tip2: Provide sensible defaults for optional values
variables.tip3: Group related variables together in your resource

structured.title: Structured Resources (JSON/YAML)
structured.description: Structured resources use JSON or YAML format to organize complex instructions clearly. They're ideal for multi-step workflows, agents, and detailed configurations.
structured.tip1: Use JSON for programmatic use; YAML for human-readable configs
structured.tip2: Keep nesting shallow (2-3 levels max) for readability
structured.tip3: Include comments in YAML to explain complex sections
structured.tip4: Validate your JSON/YAML syntax before saving

outputOptimization.title: Output Optimization
outputOptimization.description: Guide the AI to produce exactly the format and style you need by being explicit about your requirements.
outputOptimization.lengthExample: "Keep response under 200 words" / "Provide exactly 5 bullet points"
outputOptimization.styleExample: "Use simple language suitable for beginners" / "Be technical and precise"
outputOptimization.contentExample: "Do not include personal opinions" / "Focus only on factual information"

about.title: About webmcp.land
about.description: The story behind the first-ever WebMCP resources library, created a few weeks after WebMCP was announced.
about.story1Rich: webmcp.land is a central registry for WebMCP and similar AI-enabled resources on the web. It's forked from <authorLink>@f</authorLink>'s <repoLink>prompts.chat</repoLink> system and pivots it for MCP resources, and builds on top of it.
about.story2: The webmcp.land registry was created on February 28, 2026, just a few weeks after WebMCP was first announced to the world. What began as a simple solution became the go-to resource for developers looking to discover and share MCP services.
about.openSource: Completely free and open source from day one.
about.goal1Rich: We believe in the power of sharing. Our mission is to make <bold>AI resources and MCP services shared by millions</bold>, from the community, to everyone.
about.goal2Rich: Every resource, every technique, every insight shared here belongs to humanity. That's why we chose the <licenseLink>Apache-2.0</licenseLink> license: free to use, modify, and share with proper attribution. Open knowledge for all.
about.goal3: Whether you're a student learning AI, a developer building the next breakthrough, or simply curious about what's possible: this is your registry. Use it, share it, build upon it.
about.githubStars: <link>0k GitHub stars</link>, most starred resources repository
about.usedByDevelopers: Used by thousands of developers worldwide
about.supportUsIntro: We build this as a non-commercial, Apache-2.0 project and ask for nothing in return. We've kept it running thanks to our amazing sponsors. To help me keep developing this product, please consider supporting.
about.githubSponsorsDescription: Become a $800/month sponsor on GitHub and get your logo featured on the homepage.
about.supportersDescription: Subscribe for $9.99/month to keep the community running, get a verified badge, and see your name on the wall. Cancel anytime.

whatsWebmcp.title: What's WebMCP?
whatsWebmcp.subtitle: Understanding MCP and WebMCP — the standards powering AI agent connectivity
whatsWebmcp.mcpDefinition: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an open-source standard for connecting AI applications to external systems. Created by Anthropic in November 2024, it provides a universal way for AI apps like Claude, ChatGPT, and VS Code to access data sources, tools, and workflows.
whatsWebmcp.mcpAnalogy: Think of MCP like a USB-C port for AI applications. Just as USB-C provides a standardized way to connect electronic devices, MCP provides a standardized way to connect AI applications to external systems.
whatsWebmcp.mcpArchitectureDesc: MCP follows a client-server architecture. An MCP host (like Claude Desktop or VS Code) creates MCP clients that connect to MCP servers. Communication uses JSON-RPC 2.0 over two transport types: STDIO for local servers and Streamable HTTP for remote servers.
whatsWebmcp.mcpHostDesc: The AI application that coordinates and manages one or multiple MCP clients (e.g., Claude Desktop, VS Code)
whatsWebmcp.mcpClientDesc: A component that maintains a connection to an MCP server and obtains context for the host
whatsWebmcp.mcpServerDesc: A program that provides context — tools, resources, and prompts — to MCP clients
whatsWebmcp.mcpToolsDesc: Executable functions that AI models can invoke (e.g., search, calculate, query databases)
whatsWebmcp.mcpResourcesDesc: Data sources that provide context to AI models (e.g., files, database records, API responses)
whatsWebmcp.mcpPromptsDesc: Reusable prompt templates that servers can expose to clients for specific workflows
whatsWebmcp.mcpUseCase1: AI assistants accessing your Google Calendar and Notion for personalized help
whatsWebmcp.mcpUseCase2: Claude Code generating entire web apps from Figma designs
whatsWebmcp.mcpUseCase3: Enterprise chatbots connecting to multiple databases across an organization
whatsWebmcp.mcpUseCase4: AI models creating 3D designs on Blender and printing them on a 3D printer
whatsWebmcp.webmcpDefinition: WebMCP (Web Model Context Protocol) is a W3C Community Group standard that enables browsers to expose structured tools to AI agents through the navigator.modelContext API. Jointly developed by Google and Microsoft, it was released as an early preview in Chrome 146 in February 2026.
whatsWebmcp.webmcpKeyPoint: Instead of AI agents attempting to parse HTML structures or interpret screenshots, WebMCP allows web pages to declare their capabilities as structured tools with defined schemas, parameters, and security boundaries — achieving 89% token efficiency improvement over screenshot-based methods.
whatsWebmcp.webmcpDeclarativeDesc: HTML-based tool registration using form attributes. Simple and requires no JavaScript.
whatsWebmcp.webmcpImperativeDesc: JavaScript-based dynamic tool registration with full programmatic control via navigator.modelContext.registerTool().
whatsWebmcp.webmcpSecurityConsent: Human-in-the-Loop: Core design principle requiring user confirmation for sensitive operations.
whatsWebmcp.webmcpSecurityReadOnly: Read-Only Hints: Tools can be marked as read-only to bypass confirmation for query operations.
whatsWebmcp.webmcpSecurityPermissions: Permission Model: Browser-mediated consent flows similar to existing web platform APIs (like location or camera access).
whatsWebmcp.comparisonDesc: MCP and WebMCP are complementary protocols. MCP connects AI agents to backend services; WebMCP connects them to browser-based interfaces.
whatsWebmcp.comparisonMcpUseCase: Headless API operations, CLI agents, desktop AI assistants
whatsWebmcp.comparisonWebmcpUseCase: In-browser UI interactions, web app automation, context-sensitive tools
whatsWebmcp.platformDesc: webmcp.land is a discovery platform for MCP and WebMCP services. Users register their server endpoints, and the platform discovers, indexes, and exposes their tools so others can search by use case — like finding a flight booking tool or a database query service.
whatsWebmcp.platformStep1: Register your MCP or WebMCP endpoint URL
whatsWebmcp.platformStep2: The platform discovers and indexes your tools and capabilities
whatsWebmcp.platformStep3: Others search by use case to find and connect to your services
whatsWebmcp.docsDesc: Explore guides and references for using webmcp.land.
whatsWebmcp.docsApiDesc: Search and discover MCP resources programmatically. Includes REST API, MCP server configuration, and integration examples.
whatsWebmcp.docsSelfHostingDesc: Deploy your own webmcp.land instance with customizable branding, themes, and authentication.
whatsWebmcp.docsWhatsWebmcpDesc: Learn about MCP and WebMCP — the standards powering AI agent connectivity and how webmcp.land fits in.
